reviews.review_description:
It’s a great game, and I’m actually studying it to use in a master’s research project. Would it be possible to create a classic mode on normal difficulty with only 40 white balls (without needing to dodge) for 1 minute?

I tried to find an email address, but I wasn’t successful.
good

Great game but I think there should be a feature just for doges like low hug dodges with the diffrent speed option so you don’t have to punch and dodge because I think the dodging in this game is good but I’d think I’d be nice to have just dodges

Could you also add faster red gloves for dodges pls

Good cardio training, but some things innacurate:
Gloves position at angle 10-15 degree to real hands (Quest 3)
Some pushed balls in classic mode don’t fly forward, like tracking/capturing innacurate
As I see no Options menu (to change ball colors, gloves size)
no 120 Hz support
In Wall mode needs something like average reaction time in milliseconds (very good for progress tracking)
In Wall mode nearly bad idea use white and green balls on red wall, green and white in this configuration is low contranst
Speed of red gloves is same as targets, as for me it must be faster

Enjoying this! Added to my VR 🥊workouts along with TOTF1

Ideal for warm-up or cool down.
But, tbh it will depend on price if I actually buy this, as £21.99 shows & if it’s that i’ll def not buy.
Considering TOTF was £7, that’s just takin the piss!

For £23.99 it’s very very very basic. It’s a game with 2 little modes. I thought since the name was boxing trainer there would be many different modes that covered many aspects of boxing but there’s nothing to it

First day trying. I like it, but when the glove comes at me, I know how to block, both of my gloves together, sometimes I  hear the contact of my gloves blocking,  others, nor. Advice? And at a certain point,  does it offer matches ? Sparing? Other than that, I  like it. It does get me moving and in pauses, I can switch between left and south. I like it. Cheapest price for training. Fair price, fair results. Nice.

developer_response.body:
Hi Leroy,
Thanks for the great feedback. I added instructions to game modes. Also the red gloves are to be dodged by head, not to blocked by hand.

reviews.review_description:
So far I've enjoyed playing. I'm a 2nd degree black belt in Tang Soo Do so I like games like this that I can play a little bit during my home workouts. I've already recommended it to some other martial artists who do vr. 

There are a few things I'd like to see in the future:
- the current hardest difficulty is the only one that gets my cardio up. I'd like at least one harder level.
- I can't always tell if I've dodged the glove or not. I wish it made a sound when it gets me. Just need a little more feedback from the game. 
- some training game modes that are all ball or all gloves so you can practice different skills.
-for beginners (and I know this is a lot of work so I understand if this doesn't happen or takes a while) some tutorial animations to help people learn the basic boxing stances and techniques: jab, cross, hook, uppercut, and the evasive movements

developer_response.body:
Thank you Paczok,
You provided us with a great feedback.
Some updates after considering your comments.
- Added a new level yesterday.
- Added the instructions. You cannot block the gloves, they have to be dodged.
- Will try adding a warmup learning mode as well.

reviews.review_description:
Played once and now can’t get into the game. When I click on normal game nothing happens.

Tried again and it is now working  Enjoy playing it.

developer_response.body: Thanks owl for the updated feedback

reviews.review_title: Lovin' the new target height feature!
reviews.review_description:
Now can set target height lower for stronger uppercuts and low jabs!
Great job and great update!

developer_response.body: Thanks Jake for the recommendation. Glad you enjoy it.

reviews.review_description: Definitely not staring at boobys

reviews.review_title: Like walking into a gym and just getting
reviews.review_description:
busy.   I enjoy this game for its near instant loading, straightforward play, and that there is no music so I can play my own in the background.  

When I want a quick warm up session for VR play or an activity in the real world, I reach for this.  Or, I can crank up my own background music and do this workout for a good hour.

I do like the full experience games, it is also great to have an option like this one. It is stable, has never crashed or glitched.

If its possible to provide a mixed reality mode, and maybe a few more moves like others have mentioned, change little else and this is a game that has its place for what it is.  I appreciate it.  Thank you!

developer_response.body:
Hi Moebius,
Thank you very much for the positive feedback.

reviews.review_description: Its very good game for reaction and some warm-up training, but its unfinished. I hope devs will create perfect game out of this. Please add some moves like uppercut etc. and dodging, it would be cool to play only by hand tracking without controllers. Map is looking good but empty, i would prefer some big hall or training center, zen garden or something more visualy comforting/interesting. Good luck and have a great day. ;)

developer_response.body:
Thank you Chytamu,
There are great suggestions and we will consider them. Also note that dodging was added in the new update.

reviews.review_description:
Save yourself some GB and time and go buy another game. The UI is basic - just a canon throwing balls at you. 
If you want some boxing app try x-fitness, les mills body combat which are one time pay only but they work and have music. 

This game is empty. It has nothing. It looks like a child programmed it. Maybe in the future it will get updates, a better look and physics dinamics, but for now I wasted more time  for installing it and deleting it and writing this review than actually playing it because it’s nothing in there to do.
First day was a little confusing first I thought that you had to weave between the fists but I had to block   ￼

This is exactly what I needed for a bit of exercise. 
I do have a suggestion that could be added at some point, and that is that there could be an option to have the punching objects to be the size of tennis balls and the harder you punch them the more points are given.
Thank Andy

developer_response.body:
Hi Andy,
Thanks for the suggestion and great to hear you're enjoying the game. We will definitely work on the option to reduce the size of the ball for higher difficulties.

reviews.review_description:
Great boxing trainer.
Message to developer:
Would you be able to increase red gloves in rounds?
Could you make the target balls not fly so high up in the air?  Or maybe allow medications to height of the player.
Overall fun, worth buying.

A good app for maintaining physical activity. Simple and convenient. Unfortunately, I found two shortcomings in the interface: firstly, the stop button is located so that it is often pressed accidentally (it is advisable to move it back from the active zone) and secondly, after pressing stop, you need an option to continue without starting the round again. I will be glad if you can make these changes. Otherwise, an excellent program.

developer_response.body:
Hi Slava,
Thanks for your great feedback.
I moved the stop button a bit far back on v24. You can update now.
The reason we stop the game completely is to remove unfair advantage to gain higher scores. For example, to have a break and return. Otherwise, there is no technical difficulty.
Thanks again.

reviews.review_description:
This boxing app misses the mark in several key areas.  The core boxing experience feels underwhelming, lacking the impact and engagement found in other apps. While the free price point is appealing, it's overshadowed by the app's shortcomings.
The description sets unrealistic expectations, particularly regarding the "breathtaking scenery" which is nowhere to be found.  Technically, the punch physics feel weak and unresponsive, detracting from the sense of realism.  Furthermore, the app offers no customization or options to tailor the experience.
Despite these issues, I hope the developers continue to improve the app with future updates.  Adding features like varied environments, more realistic punch physics, and more customization options  would significantly enhance its appeal.

developer_response.body:
Hi Sbaday,
I am seeing that you were playing an earlier version (v16). Please update your game to receive the recent improvements on physics and performance.
I hope that you give the game another try.

reviews.review_description: I am new to VR and found your game to be very very good good, graphics good, levels good, knocks other ones I've seen out of the park well done. I can agree with others if you could cater for upper cuts hooks and avoidance you would have a brilliant game. Thanking you Chris

developer_response.body:
Thank you Chris,
I'm so glad you're enjoying the game. I will improve it in the coming weeks.

reviews.review_description: Hi! I saw your small letter in the game when i opened it. I would like to say I actually really like it, i think that some things u should do tho, is have specific points or lines the targets follow and have random generation for it, mainly because i have issues with it either going to far out or touching the ground. I love the idea of oponate type of boxing, and was also thinking that another thing u could do is, make targets that arent meant to be hit instead dodged and they go twords your head as a suggestion for adding a little more action to it, to make things a little more challenging! But yeah i really love the game so far and the idea behind it! When i dound it i suggested it to my friend and when he tried it he said he really liked it! I hope this review helps! God bless u, and have a great day/night!

developer_response.body:
Thank you,
Great points and I will reduce the height range and try to add negative obstacles.
I really appreciate the feedback.

reviews.review_description: Not even joking I’ve been playing this everyday for practice. The only thing I ask for is for more things to do for practice like a punching bag or something like that. But overall good game for practice.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much,
It's so good to hear that you're enjoying this game.

reviews.review_description: The biggest thing that will make me unintstall an app is if there is no way to close it.  This app has the potential to be fun and good exercise.  But the second time I played something happened and all of the balls started coming out at floor level.  I don't want to play on my hands and knees.  I was doing a 10 minute game, so I had to stand there because none of the controller button allowed me to stop the game.  Then when the game ended, I had to power off the headset to get out.

developer_response.body:
Hi Dave,
I'm sorry to hear you had problems with the game.
To Close: There is a close button on the v24 of the app. But you can always use the flat button on the right controller in any game or app to close it.
To pause the game: There is red button to the right.
About target: the objects go directly toward the face. If you remove the headset, that may cause the balls to go toward the ground.
I hope you give this the game another try and improve the rating. Thanks

reviews.review_description: So I saw the weather last forgive me and the bad reviews is not is not good because this game is it overall very fun game I’m talk soon and sometimes coaches out so it’s pretty good though
It was pretty good at first but then everything went broken. The ball push me off the map a lot of times. Ngl (not gonna lie) it's kinda boring......

developer_response.body:
Hi Monkey,
No one has reported this issue. Can you try the game with new updates and give us an update

reviews.review_description: Essentially this is just a PoC that still needs to be developed into a game. There is nothing into it: no options, no variety. One could wonder how it got accepted as a "game" in the store.

developer_response.body: There are options and variety. You should give it another try. But regardless, this is a free game and is very much inspired from the existing competitors.

reviews.review_title: Not Trying To Be Rude Butttt.....
reviews.review_description:
1 it's not really put together well, there's technically not really a menu per say, it just throws you in and you have a few difficulty and length of "training" settings. That's it, it's the entire game.

2 if you're looking for a boxing training game, this definitely is not what you want, if you want something better maybe I'd suggest something like fitXR or even creed rise to glory, the second one isnt really training per say but it's a good well put together boxing game.

Obviously I assume their going to continue working on this game but maybe don't publish it if it's not put together almost at all. I got this because I saw there was 5 stars and 1 stars and then no reviews and was curious if it was any good. I have come here to get the game, and leave an honest review and I have.

To the creator(s) that responded to this. I never in my comment said this wasn't a training game, I am very well aware it is a training game. I was just leaving my honest review on how it is right now, and suggesting
 (possibly while they wait on this to be worked on) other training games that will help lots better. I'm just trying to help people when they are choosing a game for training.

developer_response.body:
Hey Fox,
Please note that this comment is on an early version (version 8) of this game. Game logic, environment and performance have been updated substantially since then (now at version 30).
Please update the game and hopefully update your comment.

reviews.review_description: Could do with instructions as to do with the boxing gloves coming towards you. Apart from that, it's good for a warm-up or cool down exercise

developer_response.body:
Thank you Christo.
I added the instructions. Glad you are enjoying it.

reviews.review_description: The game cane with no instructions.  What are we supposed to do with the red gloves? Duck? I'm post surgery, so this is good to get me moving again.

developer_response.body:
Thank you LalaB,
Added the instructions recently. Hope you get better.
